title: Incident Manager — Scenario script (DSL v1)
description: Format of the `scenarios.script` JSONB column in the incident-manager-server database. Defines a UI-driven workflow that the operator follows when handling an incident. Platform-independent: Web/iOS/Android runners render the same script. See dsl-v1-draft.md for prose and rationale.

dslVersion.description: Version of THIS schema (DSL contract), not of the scenario itself. v1 = '1.0'. Used by runners to refuse unknown future versions safely.

locale.description: BCP-47 language tag in which inline `view.label`, `options[].label` etc. are written. v1: single-language scenarios. v2 reserves `labelKey` for dictionary-based i18n.

metadata.description: Identity and human-readable metadata of the scenario. The first three fields (scenarioGuid, version, name) are REQUIRED — they uniquely identify which scenario this is and which revision. Additional fields are allowed (additionalProperties: true) so the configurator and analytics can extend metadata without bumping the DSL version.

scenarioGuid.description: Stable cross-environment identifier of the scenario. UUID (preferably UUIDv7 — sortable by creation time). Generated by the configurator at scenario creation; NEVER changes for the lifetime of the scenario, including across versions. Same value as `scenarios.guid` on the server.

version.description: Monotonically growing integer revision. Bumped by the configurator on explicit 'Save as new version' (manual versioning, like git tags). Two scenarios with the same scenarioGuid but different version are considered different revisions of the SAME scenario; the server stores them as separate immutable rows in the (guid, version)-keyed `scenarios` table.

name.description: Human-readable name shown in scenario lists, breadcrumbs, audit logs. May be edited freely without bumping `version` (it is presentation, not identity). Display-only — never use as an identifier.

description.description: Optional long-form description shown in the configurator's scenario card. Not shown to operators at runtime.

tags.description: Optional flat list of tags for filtering in the configurator (e.g. ['vms', 'perimeter', 'library']).

createdAt.description: Optional. ISO-8601 timestamp of when version 1 of this scenarioGuid was created. Informational only — server has its own audit columns.

updatedAt.description: Optional. ISO-8601 timestamp of when this particular version was published. Informational only.

author.description: Optional. Free-form author identifier (email, team name, login). Informational only — server uses `creator_user_id`.

initialStepId.description: Id of the first step shown to the operator when an Incident is created from this script.

steps.description: All steps in the scenario, addressable by id. Order in the array does not matter — the graph is built via `transitions`.

timers.description: Per-scenario timers. Both are durations in seconds, NOT absolute timestamps (those are computed at Incident creation time).

escalateAfterSec.description: If the Incident is not finished within this many seconds, server emits `escalate` event. Maps to legacy IM/ `escalate_time`.

maxDurationSec.description: Hard limit. After this many seconds the Incident is force-closed (status driven by server, e.g. `closed` with resolution `timeout`). Maps to legacy IM/ `max_time`.

concurrency.description: Hints to the SERVER about how multiple operators may interact with Incidents created from this script. Runners ignore this section.

stepLockable.description: If true, server prevents two operators from working on the same Incident simultaneously. Maps to legacy IM/ `locker`.

allowMultitasking.description: If true, an operator may have several active Incidents from this script at once. Maps to legacy IM/ `multitasking`.

ScenarioGuid.description: Lowercase canonical UUID. Server uses UUIDv7 (creation-time-sortable); the schema accepts any valid UUID variant for cross-environment compatibility.

ScenarioVersion.description: Integer revision starting from 1. Monotonic per scenarioGuid; never reused or skipped.

StepId.description: Stable identifier of a step. snake_case, ASCII, max 64 chars. Used as key in scenarioResult.state and as goto target. Must be unique within the script.

OptionId.description: Stable identifier of an answer option. Stored in scenarioResult; never replaced by index.

Label.description: Human-readable text shown to the operator. v1: inline string. v2 may add a sibling field `labelKey` referencing a translation dictionary.

Option.description: Single answer option for RadioButton / Checkbox / Select.

hint.description: Optional explanatory text shown next to the option.

Step.description: Discriminated union over `type`. Each variant defines its own `view` shape. 7 interactive step types.

StepCommon.description: Fields shared by every step type. Not a standalone schema — embedded into each *Step via allOf.

editable.description: If true, operator may revisit this step and change the answer after moving forward. If false, the answer is locked once given. Maps to legacy IM/ `editable`.

RadioButtonStep.description: Single-choice from a fixed list. Operator picks exactly one option.

required.description: If true, transitions cannot fire until the operator has selected something.

layout.description: Hint to the runner. Native platforms (iOS/Android) may ignore.

CheckboxStep.description: Multi-choice from a fixed list. Operator picks zero or more options.

required.description: If true, at least one option must be selected.

SelectStep.description: Single-choice from a long list, rendered as a dropdown / picker. Use RadioButton for short lists (<= 5).

CommentStep.description: Free-form text input. Maps to legacy IM/ Helper_comment.

readonly.description: Step is shown but cannot be edited (display-only). Maps to legacy IM/ `read_only`.

minRows.description: Hint for input height in lines. Distinct from minLength (chars). Maps to legacy IM/ `min_height` (one of its meanings).

ImageStep.description: Image attachment(s). Source determines where the image comes from. Picker UX is the runner's responsibility.

source.description: camera = snapshot from a configured camera; map = screenshot of the map at the incident location; operator = uploaded by operator (paste/file); fixed = pre-attached at design time. Maps to legacy IM/ `img_type` (0/1/2/3 numeric enum, replaced by names).

fixedSrc.description: Required when source = `fixed`. URL of the pre-attached image.

allowMultiple.description: If true, operator may attach several images. Maps to UserControlImage._images list in IM/.

DatetimeStep.description: Date and/or time picker. Maps to legacy IM/ Helper_datetime.

kind.description: Maps to legacy IM/ `datetime_type` (0/1/2 numeric enum, replaced by names).

min.description: ISO-8601 lower bound. Format depends on `kind`.

max.description: ISO-8601 upper bound. Format depends on `kind`.

ButtonStep.description: Action button. Has no answer value of its own — pressing the button triggers transitions. Maps to legacy IM/ Helper_button (with `is_finish` / `is_report` replaced by explicit Actions on the transition).

Transitions.description: What happens after the operator finishes interacting with this step. First-match semantics: rules are evaluated top-to-bottom, the first whose `when` is truthy wins. If none match, `default` fires.

goto.description: Step to navigate to. `null` = stay on current step (rare, used together with side-effecting actions).

actions.description: Optional side effects executed when this rule fires (in array order, sequentially).

RuleOutcome.description: Same shape as Rule's body but without `when`. Used for non-default rules in `transitions.rules[]`. The `default` outcome has stricter requirements — see DefaultOutcome.

DefaultOutcome.description: The mandatory `default` outcome of `transitions`. Stricter than RuleOutcome: a scenario must terminate explicitly. The default branch is REQUIRED to either (a) point to a next step via `goto`, or (b) include an explicit `{type: "finish"}` action. A `default` with neither is treated as a 'dangling end' and is invalid. See dsl-v1-draft.md §7.

anyOf.description: Branch (a): goto explicitly set (including null = stay).
Branch (b): no goto, but actions[] contains at least one finish.

JsonLogicExpr.description: JSONLogic expression, evaluated against `{ state: { stepId: { value: ... } } }`. See https://jsonlogic.com — DSL v1 whitelist of operators is documented in dsl-v1-draft.md §7.

Action.description: Side effect on a transition. Discriminated union over `type`. Unknown types are ignored by the runner with a warning (Airbnb GP convention).

CallMacroAction.description: Invokes an Intellect MACRO by id. Maps to legacy IM/ `macro.{i}` and `default_macro`.

params.description: Free-form parameters passed to the macro.

FinishAction.description: Marks the Incident as finished. Maps to legacy IM/ `is_finish`. Resolution code is server-defined.

resolution.description: Optional resolution code, e.g. `processed`, `rejected`, `false_alarm`. Server may map to its own status.

GenerateReportAction.description: Generates a report from the Incident state. Maps to legacy IM/ `is_report`.

templateId.description: Optional report template identifier. If absent, server picks a default.

EscalateAction.description: Escalates the Incident to another operator/group. Server applies its own routing rules. Maps to legacy IM/ `/Escalate` API.

to.description: Optional explicit operator/group id. If absent, server applies routing.

AssignAction.description: Re-assigns the Incident to a specific operator. Maps to legacy IM/ `/Assign` API.
